title: Session 24 Meta-Analysis

session_waves.156: AE severity gradient — fatal 72.8%F vs mild 48.8%F, sudden death 94.6%F
session_waves.157: Polypharmacy patterns — 48K drug pairs, immunosuppressant cluster 90% agreement
session_waves.158: Signal confidence scoring — perfect monotonicity rho=1.0, top 10% are 97.2%F
session_waves.159: Anatomical dimorphism — 5/7 expectations reversed, cardio 64.3%F not male
session_waves.160: Drug maturity — 14.3pp mean female shift with evidence, 65% drugs shift female-ward
session_waves.161: KG centrality — degree weakly predicts sex bias rho=0.09
session_waves.162: Bootstrap stability — ALL 4 core findings 100% stable
session_waves.164: Biologic vs small molecule — mAbs 47.4%F near neutral, ICIs 73-83%F
session_waves.166: Top 50 drugs — ranitidine 93.5%F, opioids dominant female, docetaxel 11.2%F
session_waves.168: Two-axis validation — volume axis confirmed, strength gradient discrepancy found
session_waves.169: Ranitidine anomaly — 93.5%F despite F:M=1.05, 48 cancer signals

key_discoveries: Severity gradient: fatal AEs 72.8%F vs mild 48.8%F (24pp gradient)
Immunosuppressant pairs: prednisone+rituximab 401 shared AEs, 90.5% direction agreement
Confidence scoring: composite score achieves perfect monotonicity (rho=1.000) with 35.3pp gradient
Anatomical expectations reversed: cardiovascular 64.3%F (expected male), dermatological 43.9%F (expected female)
Evidence-direction shift: 65.1% of drugs shift toward female with more evidence (+14.3pp mean)
Bootstrap stability: all 4 core findings (female predominance, anti-regression, strength gradient, effect asymmetry) 100% stable
Steroid route effect: systemic steroids 70-82%F vs topical/inhaled 33-47%F
ICI sex disparity: checkpoint inhibitors 74.6%F, PD-L1 most female (82.8%F), thyroid irAEs paradoxically male
Opioid receptor effect: mu-agonists 78.3%F vs partial/antagonists 61.2%F
Ranitidine anomaly: 93.5%F despite near-parity F:M reporting ratio (1.05) — strongest evidence against reporting bias explanation
Two-axis model discrepancy: paper's strength gradient inflated (23.9pp paper vs 7.4pp actual)
Male reversal in opioids: buprenorphine+naloxone flips to 31.1%F
